verdict_rationale: Every CRITICAL/MAJOR finding from the prior audit is closed or substantively reduced; remaining items are WARNING/INFO with documented mitigations.
remediation_diff_summary: Phase 1 closed the canonical-equality gap (canonicalize/canonical_equal/save_canonical normalises tools/disallowed_tools, name/description whitespace, body CRLF/LF) and added a real fsync-based atomic_write_text plus a quarantine path for load_state/load_canonical. Phase 2 introduced a FileLayout Protocol with probe_check_path/tolerates_missing_config_key. Phase 3 added per-pair generation as a monotonic tiebreaker for mtime_wins, replaced Syncer.sync_once's bare int with a SyncResult dataclass exposing changed/failed/blocked, and made AgenticToolState.path a Path. Phase 4 split discovery.py into walker + enumerator + adoption_planner + collision_blocker mixins.
detailed_analysis: The slice is structurally sound. The discovery module — previously 507 lines as one God Module — is a five-file package (walker 103, enumerator 256, adoption_planner 128, collision_blocker 135, __init__ 12) with each file owning a single responsibility. The DiscoveryWalker is composed of three orthogonal mixins. sync.py shrank to 296 lines whose three top-level methods each delegate to ≤40-line helpers. canonical.py grew from 80 to 188 lines, every added byte load-bearing: canonicalize is the documented choke point, save_canonical runs it before serialising, canonical_equal is the public predicate. state.py shipped two durability fixes — atomic_write_text uses os.O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC + os.fsync(fd) + os.replace + _fsync_directory; staging file name carries pid + uuid4 so two writers cannot collide. load_state and load_canonical no longer collapse five failure modes into 'return {}'; they explicitly differentiate Absent vs Wrong-schema-version vs Corrupt, routing Corrupt through _quarantine_corrupt with an ERROR log naming the quarantine path. CustomizationArtifactState carries a generation counter advanced atomically with last_modified by bump(). Remaining concerns are warnings. (1) FileLayout Protocol was introduced but its method set is limited to probe_check_path and tolerates_missing_config_key — isinstance(io.file_layout, SharedKeyedMapLayout) still appears in 15 sites across discovery (4), rendering (5), adoption (4), and sync (1). (2) The (path, slot) primitive is still threaded as two separate parameters. (3) Syncer._blocked_pair_ids is reset on every sync_once entry but lives as an instance attribute. (4) Round-trip asymmetry (parse(render(c)) == c) still enforceable only by external integration tests. (5) TOCTOU between sha256_file and stat() in _add_agentic_tool_artifact: failure is now observable via SyncResult.failed but the race exists.

findings.description: FileLayout Protocol limited to probe_check_path/tolerates_missing_config_key. Fifteen isinstance(io.file_layout, SharedKeyedMapLayout) sites remain across discovery (4), rendering (5), adoption (4), and sync (1). A third FileLayout subclass would still force coordinated edits across four modules.
findings.recommendation: Extend the Protocol with polymorphic read_slot(path, slot)/write_slot(path, slot, text)/render_target(canonical, root, io) methods so callers can delete the isinstance dispatch.

findings.description: (path: Path, slot: str | None) still threaded as two fields through AgenticToolInfo, PlannedTarget, RenderResult, AgenticToolState. The slot-is-str-when-keyed-map-else-None invariant is asserted at four sites by reading both fields. Path-vs-str inconsistency closed; conceptual ArtifactLocation value object not introduced.
findings.recommendation: Introduce ArtifactLocation = (path, slot) as a frozen dataclass with a slot_aware_collision_key() method; defer until FileLayout polymorphism (CQ-14) lands.

findings.description: Syncer._blocked_pair_ids is reset on every sync_once() entry but lives as an instance attribute. A second concurrent sync_once() call on the same Syncer would observe a partially-rebuilt set. Single-threaded contract is implicit.
findings.recommendation: Either thread blocked_pair_ids as a local variable through the call chain, or document the single-threaded contract on the Syncer class and add an assert-on-reentry guard.

findings.description: Adoption planning still derives the target purely from canonical['name'] via slugify; nothing checks parse(render(canonical_for_target_tool)) == canonical. The canonical_equal() helper exists but is not invoked at the adoption boundary.
findings.recommendation: On adoption, after rendering each target, parse the result and assert canonical_equal(canonical, parsed) — log ERROR and skip the target on mismatch.

findings.description: _add_agentic_tool_artifact still calls path.stat().st_mtime unguarded after sha256_file(path). If the file is removed between digest and stat, FileNotFoundError bubbles. The handler now records the pair_id in failed[] (CQ-09), so the failure is observable, but the race could be eliminated by digesting via an open fd and statting fstat(fd).
findings.recommendation: Open the file once, fstat() it and digest from the same fd; or wrap stat() in try/except OSError and treat the artifact as removed for this poll.

findings.description: _discover_shared_keyed_map / _add_keyed_map_slot_artifact / _add_agentic_tool_artifact still carry the (pairs, blocked_pair_ids, state) trailing parameter trio.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the trio in a DiscoverySession dataclass passed once per discover() call.
